reforms.description: The 2022 amendment to the Saltsjöbaden Agreement significantly increased workers’ rights to go on paid education leave under the new Student Finance for Transition and Retraining Programme, which is largely government-financed and aims to support workers affected by structural changes.
reforms.component_name: Student Finance for Transition and Retraining Programme
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances the skills of the workforce, particularly for those at risk of unemployment, thereby improving employability and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has quickly become popular across a wide range of professions.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in adult education policy, significantly enhancing access to education for workers, which is crucial for adapting to technological changes and labor market demands.
reforms.source_quote: The 2022 amendment to the Saltsjöbaden Agreement revolutionised adult education and training in Sweden by significantly increasing workers’ rights to go on paid education leave.
